title: Investment Advisers (IA)
description:
Investment Advisers provide personalised investment advice to clients for a fee. In India, anyone who provides investment advice for a fee must be registered with SEBI under the SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations 2013. This applies to individual advisers, firms, and organisations.

The unregistered investment adviser problem in India is massive. India has over 1 lakh social media finfluencers giving investment advice — the vast majority without SEBI IA registration. Many charge subscription fees for WhatsApp groups, Telegram channels, or online courses giving stock recommendations. This is illegal under SEBI regulations.

SEBI has taken significant enforcement action against unregistered IAs in recent years, including actions against prominent social media personalities. However, with AI-generated content allowing anyone to appear as a credible financial expert, the problem continues to grow.
key_facts: All entities charging fees for investment advice must be SEBI IA registered
Individual IAs must have minimum qualification of graduate degree plus financial certification
IAs must do formal risk profiling of every client before advice
IAs must maintain proper records of advice given
IAs cannot receive commissions from products they recommend
India has over 1 lakh unregistered finfluencers giving investment advice illegally
SEBI has BASL (BSE Administration and Supervision Ltd) to oversee IAs
red_flags: Adviser charges any fee for investment advice without SEBI IA registration
Finfluencer promotes specific stocks or funds in exchange for payment from issuers
Adviser promises guaranteed returns on recommended portfolio
No formal risk profiling or KYC conducted before advice
Advice delivered through WhatsApp group or Telegram channel for subscription fee
Adviser cannot provide SEBI IA registration number on request
Promises of extraordinary returns from advice — 10x, 20x in months
No formal advisory agreement or engagement letter provided
Adviser receives commissions from products they recommend without disclosure
Advice is generic and not personalised to your financial situation
Past performance claims without SEBI compliant track record disclosure
Pressure to act immediately on recommendations without time to think
how_to_verify: Go to sebi.gov.in → Registers → Investment Advisers
Search the adviser name or firm name
Verify SEBI IA registration number and validity date
Check BASL registry for IA registrations
Ask the adviser directly for their SEBI registration certificate
Verify their registration on SEBI website before paying any fee
Check SEBI enforcement orders for any actions against the adviser
Call SEBI helpline 1800 266 7575 to verify IA registration
real_case: SEBI has taken action against multiple prominent social media personalities and Telegram channel operators charging subscription fees for investment tips without SEBI IA registration. In several cases, the operators were also receiving undisclosed commissions from companies whose stocks they were recommending — a practice known as paid promotion or pump and dump.

quiz.question: A popular Instagram finfluencer charges Rs 5000/month for a stock tips group but has no SEBI registration. This is:
options: Legal because they are popular and successful
Legal if they add disclaimers
Illegal — charging fees for investment advice requires SEBI IA registration
Legal for social media — regulations do not apply online
quiz.explanation: Charging fees for investment advice without SEBI IA registration is illegal regardless of platform, popularity, or disclaimers.

quiz.question: Under which regulation must all Investment Advisers be registered?
options: SEBI (Portfolio Managers) Regulations 2020
SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations 2013
SEBI (Research Analysts) Regulations 2014
SEBI (Mutual Funds) Regulations 1996
quiz.explanation: All Investment Advisers must be registered under SEBI (Investment Advisers) Regulations 2013.

quiz.question: What must a legitimate Investment Adviser do before giving personalised advice?
options: Show their past returns record
Do formal risk profiling and KYC of the client
Get a written guarantee from the client
Collect fee upfront
quiz.explanation: Formal risk profiling and KYC are mandatory before personalised advice. Without this, the advice is not regulatory compliant.

quiz.question: Can a SEBI registered IA receive commissions from mutual funds or products they recommend?
options: Yes — it is standard practice
Yes — if disclosed to the client
No — IAs cannot receive product commissions. They can only charge advisory fees from clients
Yes — up to 1% commission is allowed
quiz.explanation: SEBI registered IAs cannot receive product commissions. They operate on a fee-only model to avoid conflict of interest.

quiz.question: A Telegram channel has 50,000 subscribers and charges Rs 999/month for daily stock recommendations. They have no SEBI registration. What should you do?
options: Subscribe — large subscriber base means they are reliable
Subscribe for 1 month to test their tips
Do not subscribe — report to SEBI as unregistered IA
Subscribe but do not follow all tips
quiz.explanation: Operating a paid stock tips service without SEBI IA registration is illegal. Report to SEBI at sebi.gov.in/enforcement.

quiz.question: What is the minimum qualification required for an individual to become a SEBI registered Investment Adviser?
options: Class 12 pass with 5 years experience
Graduate degree plus a financial certification (like CFP, CFA, or NISM)
Any degree with 10 years experience
CA or MBA mandatory
quiz.explanation: SEBI requires minimum graduate degree plus financial certification for individual IA registration. This ensures advisers have minimum knowledge.

quiz.question: A SEBI registered IA gives you advice that results in losses. What are your options?
options: You have no recourse — all investments carry risk
File complaint with SEBI and approach BASL for redressal
Sue the IA in consumer court only
Accept the loss as market risk
quiz.explanation: You can file complaints with SEBI and approach BASL (BSE Administration and Supervision Ltd) which oversees IA conduct and has a grievance redressal mechanism.

quiz.question: What is BASL in the context of Investment Advisers?
options: Bureau of Asset and Securities Licensing
BSE Administration and Supervision Ltd — the body that oversees SEBI registered IAs
Board of Accredited Securities Licensees
Bureau for Adviser Standard Licensing
quiz.explanation: BASL (BSE Administration and Supervision Ltd) is the body designated by SEBI to supervise and oversee registered Investment Advisers.

quiz.question: An IA says: 'My recommendations have given 40% returns consistently for 5 years.' How should you evaluate this claim?
options: Accept it — 5 years is a long track record
Ask for SEBI-compliant audited track record disclosure before believing any performance claims
Invest immediately — consistent track records are rare
Ask two other clients to confirm
quiz.explanation: Performance claims must be backed by SEBI-compliant audited disclosures. Never invest based on unverified verbal claims.

quiz.question: What document must a SEBI registered IA provide before starting advisory services?
options: A simple email confirmation
A formal advisory agreement disclosing fees, services, risks, and conflicts of interest
A WhatsApp message confirming the arrangement
A receipt for fees paid
quiz.explanation: A formal advisory agreement is mandatory. It must disclose fees, services offered, risks, and any conflicts of interest.
